summary: County Commission denied rezoning for a crypto mining data center near Tullahoma. Residents said noise would be unbearable and damage property values.
Cascade Locks Port Authority canceled plans for a data center in Oregon.

opposition_groups: Coalition to Protect Prince William County
summary: A judge voided the $24.7B rezoning of 2,100+ acres for data centers near Manassas Battlefield. Virginia Court of Appeals barred construction pending appeal.
Board of Supervisors approved Amazon's rezoning 4-3 despite Planning Commission voting 5-4 against. Residents sued alleging illegal spot-zoning. Brandy Station $12B proposal also denied.
Tract withdrew its $14B rezoning application for a 1,000-acre data center complex after pushback from Goodyear and Buckeye over incompatibility with land uses.

Tract withdrew its $14B 'Project Range' application for a 30-building, 5.6M sq ft data center campus near Goodyear after grassroots opposition and municipal pushback blocked the required rezoning of ~1,000 acres.
Provident Realty withdrew its $1.3B data center proposal for a former golf course after the town council stated it could 'never support this project' at its current scale.
DeKalb County approved a 100-day moratorium on data center applications and permits. Later extended to June 2026.
North Tonawanda banned new crypto mining operations for 2 years.
Over 100 people attended the Planning Commission meeting with 20 speaking against a proposed 7-building, 102-acre data center campus. The developer announced plans to revise.
Atlanta City Council banned data centers along the Beltline corridor.
Fairfax County tightened data center rules with restrictive zoning ordinance.
Thorndale passed a formal resolution opposing a Bitcoin mining operation.
Peculiar reversed zoning for a data center after community outcry, removing data centers from permitted uses.
Gilmer County enacted a full ban on cryptocurrency mining operations.
Milam County imposed location restrictions on crypto/data center operations.
Marana approved an ordinance prohibiting municipal drinking water for data center cooling. Developers must secure alternate water sources and meet noise, energy, and environmental requirements.

Oliver County adopted a 3-year moratorium on data centers and cryptocurrency mining after over 200 people packed a public meeting in Center, ND. The zoning board voted 4-3 to recommend the moratorium.
Amazon's campus less than 200 yards from Idlebriar subdivision transformed conditions — dust, traffic, noise, inability to sell homes. Amazon offered free fiber internet and car washes as mitigation.
Sierra Club appealed Planning Commission approval, arguing the city established zoning guidelines without public input. Commissioner Becerra advocated for comprehensive data center ordinance.

opposition_groups: Coalition for Responsible Data Center Development
summary: Tract received approval for a 708MW data center that would double the city's water consumption. Castle Rock Township sued for breach of the Orderly Annexation Agreement. Residents discovered officials signed NDAs.

OpenAI's Stargate project — announced by President Trump as a $500B AI infrastructure initiative — is building its first campus on 875 acres in Abilene. The facility will draw up to 1.2 GW of power across eight 500,000 sq ft 'AI factory' halls. A $500M on-site Lancium natural gas power plant will pollute the surrounding community, putting residents at increased risk of asthma and respiratory disease. The project has sparked a housing crisis — thousands of construction workers flooded the city, driving up rents and forcing vulnerable people onto the streets. Despite $100B+ in investment, only 357 permanent jobs are promised (avg salary $57,600). Crusoe received an 85% property tax abatement on billions of infrastructure, causing Abilene to forgo enormous potential revenue. Former school board member Samuel Garcia was among the few vocal opponents questioning water and power impacts.
County officials approved a $2.4B data center during a winter storm while most residents were unaware. Council members had signed NDAs. Residents subsequently demanded answers.
Marshall County enacted a moratorium on data center development.
Douglas County approved a moratorium on data center development.
Loudoun County eliminated by-right data center development, requiring special permits for all new facilities.
Tucker United mobilized hundreds against a natural gas power plant and data center campus that could span 10,000 acres between Thomas and Davis. 2,000+ petition signatures.

A Meta data center spanning 715,000 sq ft under construction in Middleton Township was revealed in April 2025, anticipated operational in 2027 with 100 employees. Nearby communities preemptively enacted moratoriums in response to data center activity in northwest Ohio.
Coweta County passed a moratorium on data center development.

Conservation groups sued BLM to halt the Greenlink West transmission line being built largely to serve massive data center demand (4,000+ MW committed) in the Reno area, alleging federal environmental law violations.
Amazon abandoned plans after Minnesota regulators ruled it must get a permit for 250 diesel backup generators. Minnesota also eliminated a sales tax exemption on data center electricity.
Henrico County Board of Supervisors passed a Provisional Use Permit requirement for data centers.
Surge Development withdrew its application to rezone 775 acres of farmland for a data center near Tuttle Orchards, a century-old farm. Over 70 people signed up to speak at a public hearing.
City approved five data centers plus Google and QTS sites. Meta withdrew 35M+ gallons of water in 2024 under confidentiality. Residents pushed back against nuclear zoning plan.

Little Rock approved annexation and rezoning for a $1 billion data center under the secret code name 'Project Boar.' Planning commissioners and community groups including Arkansas Grass Roots United objected to the secrecy, lack of public input, and potential environmental impacts. Construction expected to begin Q1 2026.

Cielo Digital Infrastructure announced a $2.1 billion data center near Gaffney in Cherokee County. Residents voiced strong concerns at county council meetings about power grid strain, environmental impacts, and limited permanent jobs. The county implemented restrictions on data centers in response. A regional petition opposing Upstate SC data centers has gathered approximately 4,000 signatures.

company: University of Michigan / Los Alamos National Laboratory / DTE Energy
project_name: Hydro Park Data Center / AI Supercomputer
summary: A $1.2 billion data center project on South Hydro Park by the University of Michigan, Los Alamos National Laboratory, and DTE Energy would include a 100MW facility for classified national security AI work and a 10MW UMich research facility on 144.5 acres of critical wetland near Ford Lake Dam. The opposition group 'Stop the Data Center' organized assemblies and protests. Ypsilanti Township pushed back, urging relocation.

Oakland University's Board of Trustees approved plans for a data center on Parking Lot 35, located behind Hillcrest Residence Hall near the Native American Heritage Site, biological preserve, and nature trail. Student Congress led opposition efforts through an Action Network petition. Students and community members cite concerns about water usage, chemical pollution, and impacts on nearby dorm residents.

Millard County Commission unanimously rezoned 1,100+ acres from agricultural to heavy industrial for Creekstone Energy's 10GW data center campus despite significant public opposition arguing the rezone permanently shifts the area's rural character.
Oregon passed POWER Act requiring data centers (20+ MW) to pay full costs of electricity infrastructure rather than shifting ~$210M in upgrades to residential ratepayers. Signed by Governor Kotek.
Warrenton Town Council voted to fully ban data centers from the town.

Google is behind a data center project in Monrovia, Morgan County, IN. Despite resident protests, the Morgan County Plan Commission gave a favorable recommendation for rezoning. County commissioners approved additional land for the project. Two Change.org petitions oppose the rezoning, and the Protect Morgan County group is raising funds for legal action.
Oldham County passed a 150-day moratorium 4-2. Western Hospitality Partners subsequently withdrew its $6B, 600MW application. 'We Are Oldham County' organized fierce opposition.

project_name: AWS Data Center at 8050 Camino Arroyo
summary: Amazon Web Services received approval on July 3, 2025 for a 218,000 sq ft data center requiring 49 MW from PG&E and 25 emergency generators on a 56-acre site. Two Change.org petitions were created - one to stop the project and another to ban/regulate data centers in Gilroy. The mayor said the city cannot legally stop the project.

Mesa City Council adopted new zoning rules restricting data centers to General and High Industrial zones (4.1% of city land), requiring special use permits. Fifteen data centers had been constructed, approved, or proposed in Mesa within six years.
St. Charles enacted a full moratorium on data center development.
Despite the Planning Commission recommending denial, the Board of Trustees approved rezoning of 522 acres. Residents gathered 957 signatures to force a public referendum.
Oppidan halted its project citing lengthy permitting for diesel generators. MCEA sued the city alleging officials hid information and approved faulty environmental review.
Residents rallied against a 250MW, 6-building data center near Harris Nuclear Plant. 2,000+ petition signatures. Would consume 1M gallons of water/day.
Bessemer delayed rezoning for Project Marvel, a $14B, 700-acre campus, amid opposition from residents and environmental groups citing environmental justice. 7,000+ petition signatures.
Developer withdrew $1B rezoning application after 8 months of resident organizing. Planning Commission had backed project in May. Opposition cited industrialization of rural land.

Construction underway on $7B facility. Residents cite farmland preservation, electricity reliability, water usage, strain on volunteer emergency services. Slogans: 'No Eminent Domain,' 'My Land, My Choice.'
Community pushback growing over Musk-backed xAI supercomputer facility in southwest Memphis environmental justice community.
Microsoft filed plans for two data centers ($350M each) near Castroville. Community concerns about traffic, water usage; located within 5 miles of two elementary schools.
Tract's $30B data center proposal collapsed amid a dispute involving Dale Earnhardt's widow and son over family land use.

Vantage gained approval for a 672-acre, $15B data center campus for Oracle and OpenAI's Stargate project. Great Lakes Neighbors United filed a lawsuit challenging the approval process, alleging the council improperly met in closed session. Hundreds rallied against the project. Ozaukee County opposes the proposed transmission line routes through natural preserves.

Copia Power Devco LLC received approval (4-1 vote) from Lyon County commissioners for a Master Plan Amendment to build the Monarch Data Center - 4.6 million sq ft across 14 buildings on 505 acres in Mason Valley, Yerington. Multiple Change.org petitions oppose the project, citing water scarcity in the drought-prone region and impacts on the agricultural community.
Tucson City Council voted 7-0 to reject Project Blue, a 290-acre, 600MW data center linked to AWS. Community opposition centered on water use and energy costs. Amazon later pulled out entirely.

Wrangell's borough assembly authorized a nonbinding agreement with Greensparc to explore a small-scale data center using hydroelectric power. Community members raised concerns about noise, light, and water discharge. A Cordova resident reported Greensparc's existing facility there has been 'underwhelming and underperforming.'

Over 100 residents packed a council meeting to oppose a proposed 664-acre, 14-building hyperscale data center by NorthPoint Development in the small riverside community of fewer than 2,000 people. Projected peak power demand of 1,008 MW. No formal application has been filed but residents are organizing preemptively.

Applied Digital is developing the $3B, 280 MW Polaris Forge 2 data center in Harwood. An annexation battle erupted between Fargo and Harwood over the site. Residents raised concerns about water, power costs, noise, and environmental impacts at contentious public meetings. Fargo eventually withdrew its annexation attempt in December 2025.

opposition_groups: Empowerment Congress of Dona Ana County
summary: County Commission advanced 4-1 preliminary approval for massive data center near Santa Teresa. Community questioning water impacts, oversight, and approval speed.
Clayton County Board of Commissioners approved a moratorium on new data centers.
Troup County enacted a moratorium on data center development.
Prince George's County paused data center approvals amid policy review.
LaGrange City Council passed a 180-day moratorium on data centers.
Pike County enacted a moratorium on data center development as part of a wave of Georgia county ordinances.
Lamar County enacted a moratorium on data center development.
Compass proposed a $1B+ campus on 330 acres of farmland. Facebook group with 900+ members organized. Council ultimately approved the project unanimously.
Commissioners unanimously rejected proposal to allow data centers in office-park zones after local petition and door-to-door opposition campaign.
Lawmakers passed bill requiring quarterly public disclosure of water and electricity consumption by data center operators. Awaiting Governor Murphy's signature.

A $600M data center proposed at the Armory in St. Louis spurred a petition with 12,300+ signatures calling for a year-long moratorium. Over 200 residents attended a community-led town hall. The Board of Aldermen passed Resolution 111 (9-2) to regulate data centers, but a proposed outright ban was voted down. Opposition argues the resolution still allows projects to proceed.

A data center proposed at a former power station site in Carbon County's Packer and Banks townships has been delayed by officials. Opposition groups Save Carbon County and Greenfire Coalition have spoken out, with two petitions gathering around 900 signatures total. Concerns focus on loss of green spaces and community impacts.
Trustees unanimously approved a 9-month moratorium after residents complained about noise from existing Amazon facilities and concerns about water and electricity costs.
Neighbors questioned why a 12MW data center was located in their neighborhood, raising concerns about pollution and community impact after RadiusDC broke ground.

District 17 Council Member Markus Winkler proposed a six-month moratorium on data center approvals to give planners time to develop regulations. A survey showed 81.5% of respondents chose 'Nowhere in Jefferson County' for data center locations. However, the moratorium was tabled indefinitely in the Planning and Zoning Committee. City planners are drafting regulations.
Developers withdrew their proposal for a 209-acre, 1.5 GW data center campus after the borough denied an additional meeting and residents collected ~800 petition signatures.

The College Station City Council unanimously rejected a land sale for a 600MW data center after nearly 6 hours of discussion. Over 75 individuals registered to speak and a petition with 5,000+ signatures was submitted. Residents raised concerns about water, electricity usage, noise, and long-term health impacts. The council cited mounting public pressure as a key factor in their decision.

Google withdrew its 468-acre 'Project Flo' data center hours before the council was expected to vote it down. 15 of 25 council members announced opposition. A 7,000+ signature petition and loud cheers erupted at the withdrawal.

Google withdrew its $1B 'Project Flo' data center proposal minutes before the City-County Council vote on September 22, 2025, after months of community pushback. Hundreds attended to protest the 468-acre farmland conversion. Residents cited water/electricity consumption, environmental risks, and limited permanent jobs.
Mayor Randy Knaack announced pause in city support citing 'resident concerns over secrecy and community impacts.' City had already annexed land for the $1.6B project.

TeraWulf signed an 80-year lease for 183 acres at a former coal plant on Cayuga Lake to develop up to 400 MW of data center capacity. Hundreds of residents submitted public comments opposing the project, and the town held a public hearing on a moratorium to pause large-scale development while rewriting the zoning code.
Five residents sued over a data center on land deeded for parkland. Court temporarily blocked it but dismissed the lawsuit, ruling residents lacked standing.

Fidelis New Energy proposed a data center complex in Mason County, but residents are frustrated by the lack of answers about impacts on water, environment, and public health. State law HB 2014 stripped local control over 'high-impact' data centers and diverts property tax revenue away from local bodies. Residents from Mason, Tucker, Mingo, and Logan counties visited the Capitol in Feb 2026 to demand lawmakers restore local control.
White County enacted a moratorium on data center development.
Yorkville tabled the vote on a 1,000-acre Project Cardinal after over an hour of public opposition. Project effectively defeated at the most preliminary stage.

opposition_groups: No Data Centers in Jones County
summary: EagleRock Partners withdrew rezoning for 600+ acres after organized opposition under 'No Data Centers in Jones County.' Commissioners voted 4-1 for a 90-day moratorium.
Project secured rezoning despite opposition; currently facing legal challenges from community members.
Planning & Zoning Commission considering industrial rezoning for potential 200 MW data center. Environmental group opposing, citing lack of transparency on impacts.

The Page City Council voted 5-2 to sell 500 acres less than one mile from Horseshoe Bend to Huntley LLC for a 1GW data center. Residents filed a referendum with 409 hand-signed signatures, but the city rejected it on a technicality. Online petitions gathered over 1,800 signatures from a town of only 7,000 people.

Over 30 Carroll County residents filed a lawsuit in Superior Court challenging Villa Rica's annexation and rezoning of 200+ acres for a data center. Door-to-door petitions gathered 500 signatures with approximately 95% of respondents opposing the project. Concerns include 1.1 million gallons/day water use and proximity to residential areas.

Over 100 residents from New York and New Jersey objected to DataBank's proposal for a 146,000 sq-ft data center adjacent to Lake Tappan reservoir. The Bi-State Datacenter Crisis Coalition organized opposition citing threats to wetlands, wildlife, water quality, and fire risks from lithium-ion battery storage.

Preble County Planning Commission unanimously (11-0) denied rezoning of 300 acres of farmland for a data center. The developer withdrew his application in October after residents organized a referendum campaign. Former Republican state lawmaker Gene Krebs helped lead opposition.

California-based Prologis purchased land from the city of Trenton for 'Project Mila,' a data center project half a mile from residential homes. Residents are pushing back alongside broader Butler County opposition to multiple data center proposals.

A Fortune 100 company proposed a billion-dollar hyperscale data center near Maysville, but NDAs signed by town leaders left residents with no information. The 'Save Mason County' petition gathered 1,300 signatures and a 1,600-member Facebook group formed. The joint planning commission voted 5-1 to approve governing rules despite packed rooms of opponents.

Platte County Presiding Commissioner Scott Fricker publicly opposed the $100B 'Project Kestrel' plan for six hyperscale data centers north of KCI airport. The project would total 1.8M sq ft. Fricker cited Evergy's 13.4% rate increase driven by data center demand and questioned how much tax revenue would remain in Platte County.

project_name: Lower Mt. Bethel Data Center Campus
summary: Peron Development and J.G. Petrucci Company are proposing a data center campus at the intersection of Gravel Hill Road and Martins Creek Belvidere Highway in Lower Mt. Bethel Township, Northampton County. The site is across from the John M. Mauser Nature Education Trail System along the scenic Delaware River. A petition gathered ~4,600 signatures opposing the project.

opposition_groups: Joplin Citizens Against Wildwood Ranch Data Center Site
summary: The Joplin City Council approved annexation and rezoning of Wildwood Ranch for a data center. Opposition group launched referendum effort to repeal the decision and establish a 12-month data center moratorium. Two Change.org petitions gathered 1,400+ signatures. The group seeks to overturn the council's approval through a public vote.

Despite a petition with 2,500+ signatures and a 4,000-member opposition group, Hobart reached a deal with Amazon Web Services for a data center on 500+ acres. AWS plans to invest $15 billion in 2.4 GW of data center capacity across Northwest Indiana. The No Data Centers Hobart Indiana group has also launched a dedicated opposition website with legal and community action resources.

A Change.org petition opposes a data center project in Plainfield, Indiana, citing lack of public transparency and insufficient evaluation of how the facility will affect the local ecosystem and daily life. Part of a wave of data center opposition across Indiana.

Lordstown was one of the first Ohio municipalities to institute a moratorium on data centers. The village is now entangled in a legal battle with a developer after blocking a data center bid. A Change.org petition supports the moratorium efforts.

A petition led by resident Cassia Core gathered nearly 2,000 signatures opposing the conversion of the historic Cleveland IX Center into an AI data center. The IX Center has hosted the Cleveland Auto Show and Christmas Connection for decades. Brook Park residents are particularly vocal about noise pollution and utility price increases.

A proposal to convert the former Pennhurst State School site in Chester County into a massive data center drew 12,000+ petition signatures. The property borders private homes, the Southeastern Veterans Center, and the Schuylkill River Trail. The Board of Supervisors scrapped the data center ordinance in December 2025, with a final vote expected in March 2026.

project_name: Cleveland Cliffs Steel Plant Data Center
summary: A developer attempted to convert the Cleveland Cliffs steel plant on 66 acres of Plymouth Township's riverfront corridor into a data center for undisclosed Big Tech tenants. Over 1,000 people signed a petition opposing the project. The application was abruptly withdrawn in November 2025 due to legal issues regarding the developer's standing.

Residents of Covington and Clifton townships in Lackawanna County oppose a massive data center involving 35 structures up to 120 feet tall on 500 rezoned acres. The project would require 35 wells that could disrupt local water tables and level a square mile of forest. A county commissioner has asked the governor for a statewide moratorium.

A $7 billion data center backed by Oracle and OpenAI is planned on 575 acres of farmland north of Michigan Avenue, with 2.2 million sq ft. DTE Energy says it would require 25% more energy than the entire current system. A consent judgment secured the project's path, bypassing normal review, sparking massive backlash. A resident filed a lawsuit to stop the project.

company: Franks Investment Company LLC / SAC III Acquisition Co
summary: Multiple data center projects are advancing in the Shreveport area, including a 300+ acre Resilient Technology Park at 7340 Greenwood Road and land purchases by SAC III Acquisition Co in west Shreveport and north Bossier Parish. An Action Network petition demands a pause on negotiations and disclosure of NDAs signed by municipal officials. The Shreveport City Council has approved at least one project despite opposition.
Augusta Township residents near Milan, Ohio created a Change.org petition to stop a proposed data center from being approved in their community.
Microsoft canceled plans for a 244-acre data center citing 'community feedback' after 40 people spoke against it and a 2,000-name petition was filed.
Residents formed a joint coalition to oppose new power-line routes serving a planned Vantage Data Center in Port Washington. Pressing the Wisconsin PSC for alternative routes.

After a 5-hour public hearing with 150+ residents and a 2,500-signature petition, the Planning Commission declined to recommend rezoning. Fiscal Court unanimously blocked the proposal and adopted a moratorium.
City Council voted 4-0 to rezone 200+ acres for 'Project Loon,' but residents filed lawsuits alleging the city kept the public in the dark. A Facebook group gained over 3,500 members.
A $12B data center campus on 400 acres is on hold after a protest petition and lawsuit challenged the rezoning. Residents allege rushed review and manipulation of petition signatures.
Putnam County enacted a moratorium on data center development.
Residents packed council meetings to protest a zoning overlay allowing data centers. Council approved the amendment; residents appealed the decision.
Residents called for stricter regulatory framework for data center proposals along Route 460.

Denver-based Amp Z is negotiating a $1.05 billion AI data center at the former Southland Paper Mill site outside Lufkin city limits. While it promises 500 construction jobs, only about 30 permanent jobs would be created. A Change.org petition has gathered 1,000+ signatures opposing the project. Angelina County rejected a moratorium request.

A Change.org petition opposes high-impact data center development in Rockford, IL, demanding sustainable growth alternatives. The petition calls for open dialogue, transparent cost analysis, and investment in small businesses and workforce development rather than committing local resources to energy-intensive data centers.

A Change.org petition calls on Foristell city leaders to reject annexation and rezoning proposals tied to the development of two massive data centers on over 400 acres of undeveloped land. The petition opposes transformation of this land into industrial-scale data facilities.

Project Cumulus proposes a data center near Elm Point Road and Highway 370 in St. Charles, within a protected wellhead zone endangering drinking water. The project involves storage of over 1 million gallons of diesel fuel. St. Charles moved to ban data centers for 1 year in response to community opposition.

opposition_groups: York County Citizens for Responsible Growth
summary: QTS Realty Trust acquired 400+ acres in York County under the code name 'Project COBRA' in November 2022 and cleared the land without transparency or community consent. Residents near the site experienced bright lights, noise, and blasting from construction. A petition gathered 900+ signatures demanding the project halt until environmental and health concerns are addressed.

A regional petition opposes data center developments across Northwest Indiana, including proposals in Crown Point, Merrillville, Michigan City, Portage, Schererville, Munster, and Lowell. This reflects concern about the cumulative impact of AWS's $15 billion commitment to data center capacity in the region.

A Change.org petition demands that Kokomo prevent data centers from establishing in the city. Mayor Moore has publicly vouched to protect Kokomo from data centers, indicating strong local government support for the opposition position.
Concerned residents of South Strabane Township support a protectional and robust data center ordinance to govern any proposed mega data center development in the township.

Energy Storage Solutions plans a 'NetZero' data center in Cumberland County near three churches and many neighborhoods. Despite marketing as green energy, the facility relies on natural gas combustion. The company initially claimed 500 jobs but documents show only 69 permanent positions, mostly security and janitorial. A petition gathered 1,000+ signatures.

project_name: Tract Silver Springs / Multiple Data Centers
summary: Lyon County is moving fast on mega-data centers in the Silver Springs area, including a 1,060-acre Tract Silver Springs project with 1.6 GW capacity and Microsoft's 274-acre project. A petition raises alarm about water drawn from the same aquifers used for homes and farms in this drought-prone region.
Selectboard voted to pause conversations about a $5B data center on town-owned land after grassroots campaign by 'Protect Wiscasset.' Little info about end user or power source had been disclosed.
Board of Supervisors voted for a one-year moratorium on data center approvals in unincorporated areas, citing concerns about excessive energy and water use.

opposition_groups: Coalition Against Data Centers in South Ohio
summary: Hamilton City Council Chambers were packed on Nov. 12 with residents opposing a proposed data center requesting 240 MW of power. Resident Teri Cunningham created the Coalition Against Data Centers in South Ohio Facebook group with 600+ members. Citizens circulate petitions, create yard signs, and speak at biweekly council meetings.
Organized opposition forced withdrawal of a 2M sq ft AI data center at a retired steel mill after residents identified a legal flaw in the developer's agreement. 1,000+ petition signatures.

Nine nonprofits filed PSC complaint to stop NorthWestern Energy from shifting data center infrastructure costs to residential customers. NorthWestern signed deals totaling 75-1000MW without regulatory approval.

company: Atlas Power / Sabey Data Center Properties
opposition_groups: Butte Watchdogs for Social and Environmental Justice
summary: A coalition of nine nonprofit groups petitioned the Montana PSC to impose rules preventing NorthWestern Energy's 413,000+ residential customers from shouldering data center infrastructure costs. NorthWestern has signed LOIs with 11 data center developers for up to 1,400MW total.

opposition_groups: Stop the Data Centers Livingston County
summary: Township enacted a unanimous six-month moratorium after ~2 hours of public comment. Meta identified as the company behind the project. Developer withdrew rezoning in Dec 2025.

After CRG withdrew its data center proposal from St. Charles, the Festus City Council unanimously annexed 240 acres for data center development. At least two residents filed lawsuits; Wake Up Jeffco organized opposition. A door-to-door petition gathered 1,400 registered voter signatures calling for a public vote on whether to allow the data center.

California-based Serverfarm plans a $1B+ data center on 135 acres near I-40, adjacent to a hospital and assisted living facility. Residents launched a Change.org petition demanding the city and county pause the project until the public gets full answers on noise, pollution, water use, and cooling systems. Planning commission approved site plan despite concerns.

AWS proposed a $4B, 471-acre data center in Wilmington. 44 residents testified against the project at a January 2026 city council meeting, and the Planning Commission voted unanimously to withhold site plan approval. Amazon would pay only 30% of normal property taxes for 30 years.
Monroe County passed a data center moratorium to review potential limitations on development.
Athens-Clarke County Commission paused new data center development. The existing Athens Studios data center would need to rezone.
Cloverleaf scrapped plans in this Kewaunee County farming community near Lake Michigan. Developer was subsequently also rejected in Greenleaf.
New ordinance bans data centers in the township's airport industrial park.
Rural residents mobilizing against Infrakey's planned 526-acre facility. Opposition cites regulatory circumvention via Texas SB 2038 and water annexation concerns.

opposition_groups: Edgecombe Neighbors for Data Center Accountability
summary: Energy Storage Solutions proposed a $19B, 900MW data center on 300 acres of county land in Rocky Mount that would use up to 5M gallons of water/day during a severe drought. Community members wore red to the Dec 1 commissioners meeting, with nearly all speakers opposing the sale. The company claims 500 jobs but documents show only 69 permanent positions. The project may include on-site gas drilling and diesel generators, making it essentially an off-grid industrial power plant.

project_name: Project Matador / Trump Advanced Energy and Intelligence Campus
summary: Project Matador, potentially the largest data center on earth, is proposed 12 miles NE of Amarillo on 6,000 acres. Over 279 comments were filed with TCEQ, mostly opposing, plus 63 contested case hearing requests. Panhandle Taxpayers for Transparency challenged the water agreement. Construction was paused pending TCEQ permits. A key tenant terminated its agreement in Dec 2025. The project threatens the Ogallala Aquifer, the region's primary groundwater supply.

Sabey Data Centers proposed a $4B, 130-acre campus on the southwest side of Indianapolis. The Decatur Township Civic Council's executive committee unanimously voted against it. About 150 residents attended a panel with no public commenters in favor. Sabey filed for a variance of use that bypasses City-County Council vote. MDC hearing examiner recommended approval; final vote set for March 18, 2026.

Microsoft purchased 240 acres in the town of Paris, which Kenosha annexed. The city signed an NDA with Microsoft in May 2024 keeping the project secret. Protesters gathered in Kenosha on December 1, 2025 against the data center. Community opposition focuses on secrecy and environmental impacts.

Janesville signed an NDA for a proposed data center, sparking resident opposition. The group SNOW determined the data center would not reliably improve quality of life. Protests held in Janesville as part of statewide opposition. Community concerns center on secrecy deals and economic claims.

Meta's $1B data center in Beaver Dam became controversial when it was revealed the city signed an NDA and worked with shell companies for 14 months before the project became public. Meta announced involvement in December 2025. Residents rallied against the lack of transparency.

Gemini Capital proposed a $1.9B, 500 MW hyperscale data center in eastern Sioux Falls. The city council approved rezoning but opponents tried unsuccessfully to petition the decision to a citywide referendum. Anti-data center petitioners fell short of signatures needed for a vote.

project_name: Strata Expanse AI Center of Excellence
summary: Houston-based Strata Expanse hosted a ribbon cutting for an AI data center in the Haverhill area at 1476 County Road 1A. A petition gathered 5,178 signatures opposing the project. The nearby city of Ironton passed a data center moratorium on first reading. Community members demand environmental and community impact assessments before construction proceeds.

Muhlenberg Township (Pickaway County, near Circleville) passed a 12-month moratorium on new data centers after a petition gathered 700+ signatures. Key concerns include proximity to schools, potential for private wells to dry up, and impacts on the rural residential character of the township.

project_name: Potential Data Center near Fort Eustis
summary: A resident created a petition after word spread about a potential data center near Fort Eustis, gathering 3,500+ signatures. Residents rallied against the proposal. However, city officials state there are no conversations, plans, or negotiations underway to bring a data center to Newport News. The petition demonstrates pre-emptive community opposition.

Infrakey proposed a $10B data center on 520 acres of farmland near Elm Mott. Opposition circulated petitions gathering 3,000+ signatures, created a Facebook group ('Stop the data center on Taylor lane') and website, and held regular strategy meetings attended by state legislators. Despite a 6-1 city council vote for a nonbinding agreement, the city has not approved annexation of the site. Concerns center on water, air pollution from gas-fired plants, and noise.

Ten Mingo County residents filed a federal lawsuit against TransGas for building two data centers and two natural gas plants without adequate community input. Despite public pushback, WV DEP approved air quality permits for the energy campus. State law HB 2014 stripped local governments of authority to regulate certified data centers, leaving residents with limited recourse.

Infrakey proposed a $10B data center on 520 acres near Lacy Lakeview, north of Waco — the largest industrial development in McLennan County history. The tiny city of 7,000 would gain $50M/year in tax revenue, but rural neighbors launched a fierce opposition campaign. Since November 2025, opponents have gathered 3,000+ petition signatures, created Facebook and Instagram accounts, and held regular strategy meetings. Petition organizer Mary Beechner brought concerns to the Waco City Council. Lacy Lakeview attempted to annex the site using a Texas law allowing cities to 'lasso' territory for tax revenue.
Starke County Planning Commission unanimously approved a one-year moratorium on data centers exceeding 5,000 sq ft. Fourth Indiana county to enact a data center moratorium.

IVCM is developing a $10B, 330MW AI data center on 74 acres in unincorporated Imperial County that would consume ~30% of the local utility's power. The City of Imperial filed a CEQA lawsuit; the developer counter-sued in federal court; grassroots groups NIMBY Imperial and Valle Imperial Resiste have gathered 4,000+ signatures and organized street protests.
Green Charter Township adopted a one-year moratorium on all data center development, citing concerns about water/energy consumption and noise.
County Council voted 7-2 to deny rezoning for a $13B data center near New Carlisle after a marathon 10-hour meeting with public comment lasting until 3 AM.
County postponed 200-acre AI data center 'Project Tango' after community outrage.

Chandler City Council unanimously rejected a $2.5B AI data center. Emails opposing the project outnumbered supporters 20-to-1. Former Sen. Kyrsten Sinema lobbied for the project but the council rejected it anyway.

project_name: Bates Mill No. 3 AI Data Center
summary: City Council unanimously voted down $300M AI data center. Community mobilized over a weekend to change councilors' minds within days of the proposal going public.

opposition_groups: Arizona Center for Law in the Public Interest
summary: After Tucson City Council rejected Project Blue in August 2025, Pima County approved the $3.6B data center 3-2. The Arizona Center for Law in the Public Interest sued alleging open meeting law violations. AG Mayes also challenged the deal as unconstitutional rate-setting.
First North Carolina county to enact a one-year data center moratorium, halting applications for data centers, cryptocurrency mining, and high-impact data processing through Dec 2026.
Waterville Township trustees unanimously approved a 12-month moratorium on data centers on Dec. 17, 2025. The city of Waterville had also voted in early December for a separate 6-month moratorium.
Township suspended its participation after a venue was too small to accommodate opposition turnout. Franklin Lowell LLC withdrew its application.
Hundreds packed a Walterboro auditorium to oppose an 859-acre, 1-GW data center near the ACE Basin ecosystem. Seven state legislators formally opposed it. Landowners filed a lawsuit.
Frederick County Council passed a 2,615-acre overlay map constraining data center development to a single zone covering less than 1% of the county's landmass.
Oshtemo Charter Township is considering a preventative moratorium on data centers, citing concerns over utility rates, water use, and environmental impact.
Residents organized against 'Project Cannoli,' a 1 GW data center requiring 2M+ gallons of water daily. 1,400 petition signatures collected. Planning Commission approved 5-2.
Luther residents rallied against Beltline Energy data center, citing utility price spikes, water supply impacts, noise, and property values.
Rep. Mears introduced HB 259 to require community benefit agreements between large energy use facilities and host municipalities, mandating that utility costs cannot be passed on to other customers.

Maryland HB 120 would prohibit construction of new data centers and prohibit state or local approvals unless the General Assembly enacts legislation related to co-location with power generation. The General Assembly also overrode Gov. Moore's veto to mandate an environmental and economic impact study due September 2026.

WV House Bill 2014 stripped local control over data centers and diverted property tax revenue away from local governments. In the 2026 session, bills to restore local control and require water usage disclosure were introduced but blocked by Republican leadership. Protesters in Tucker, Mingo, Mason, and Berkeley counties united statewide.

Rhode Island legislators shifted from offering data center tax breaks to regulating them. Bills require data centers to report daily water usage and cooling methods, develop conservation plans, and prevent operators from passing electricity costs onto residential and business customers.

Limerick Town Center LLC proposes 'Project Laurel,' a 1.4 million sq ft data center campus on 192 acres near the Limerick nuclear power plant. The Pennsylvania Game Commission approved a controversial land swap of state game lands to facilitate the project, outraging residents. A petition has 680+ signatures opposing the development.

Residents of Conewago Township in York County oppose plans to rezone agricultural and residential land for AI data centers. The petition argues the use is incompatible with the surrounding area and demands the rezoning process halt until full community discussions take place.

Laramie County commissioners unanimously approved Project Jade, a 2.7GW data center campus (scalable to 10GW) south of Cheyenne, despite neighbor opposition. The $50B project could make it the largest US data center.

Applied Digital proposed a $16B AI data center campus in Toronto, a rural town of ~200 people. Over 100 people attended a town hall in Deuel County expressing concerns. A volunteer firefighter worried about emergency response capacity. Applied Digital stated the project is not viable without state tax incentives.

An estimated 150 residents stormed the Wood County Planning Commission meeting demanding a moratorium on data centers after Meta revealed plans for an $800 million data center on 280 acres in Middleton Township north of Bowling Green. Residents raised concerns about water consumption, electric usage, and environmental health. Residents also questioned Ohio's fast-tracked approval of a 350 MW 'Apollo' gas plant to power Meta's data center. Ohio law does not give counties the authority to issue moratoriums for specific land uses, limiting local options.
Saline City Council unanimously adopted a one-year moratorium, prompted by inquiries from Fortune 100 companies. City's zoning code did not yet define data centers.

Engineered Land Solutions proposed a 1,800-acre data center campus near Duke Energy's Belews Creek plant along the Dan River. Over 100 people, vastly against the project, packed the commissioners' meeting. The Planning Board voted against recommending the rezoning, but commissioners approved it 3-2 in January 2026. Opponents cite threats to indigenous cultural sites (Upper Saura Town), the Dan River watershed, and conflict with the Stokes County 2035 Land Use Plan.
Cloverleaf Infrastructure abandoned plans in the rural Brown County village after community resistance including petitions and appeals to officials.
City council passed a one-year moratorium on data centers over 10,000 sq ft. Mayor Rhodes-Conway and 10 alders led the effort. One of the nation's largest cities to enact such a ban.

opposition_groups: GASP (Greater Birmingham Alliance to Stop Pollution)
summary: The Birmingham City Council proposed a 180-day moratorium on new data center applications to study zoning, land-use, and infrastructure impacts. Public hearing held Jan 13, 2026; council vote scheduled for March 3, 2026. GASP and Alabama Rivers Alliance urged residents to support the pause.

Harvey County Commission unanimously approved a moratorium on data center construction in unincorporated areas through the end of 2028 (roughly three years). Concerns centered on water use from the Equus Beds Aquifer. Nearby Andale and Colwich residents pushed back against proposed projects.

State Sen. Steinborn introduced legislation to empower state regulators to oversee data center microgrids after Project Jupiter's applications revealed potential emissions of 14M tons of greenhouse gases per year.
Heated city council meeting over Project Mustang resulted in arrest of a resident for exceeding 3-minute speaking time. Hundreds signed petition opposing over water and infrastructure concerns.
Lawsuit to void annexation of 827 acres for Google's Project Spring, alleging city secretly annexed county land six miles outside town. Osage County residents were not notified.
S.205 would freeze data center construction until July 2030. Preemptive — Vermont has no hyperscale data centers yet but developers have begun scouting.

Sedgwick County Commissioners adopted a 90-day pause on data center applications (expiring April 17, 2026). Senate majority leader Chase Blasi called for a three-year moratorium. A flood of emails and calls from residents voiced opposition. Listening town halls were scheduled for March 2026.

LB1111 would require data centers to pay full cost of new electricity infrastructure. Responds to Meta's Sarpy County facility keeping a 1954 coal plant running, extending health risks to low-income North Omaha community.
City Council approved moratorium after years of complaints about noise, diesel fumes from Markley facility. When fully built: 27 diesel generators, 16 cooling towers.

Kansas City Council unanimously approved zoning changes reclassifying data centers as industrial facilities, limiting them to specific zoning districts, revoking by-right status for larger data centers, and requiring City Council approval and will-serve letters from water and electric utilities.
Planning Commission voted 4-1 to deny $800M Project Atlas rezoning after 3+ hour meeting. Despite denial, proposal goes to city council in April.
City Council unanimously adopted 45-day moratorium after hundreds packed City Hall. Now drafting ballot measure for permanent ban. HMC has threatened litigation.

Naperville City Council voted 6-1 to deny Karis Critical's proposal for a 211,000 sq ft data center on the former Nokia/Lucent campus. A Change.org petition gathered 5,000+ signatures. Originally proposed as two data centers, the developer reduced to one after community opposition. Located near residential areas, Danada Forest Preserve, and a sensory playground for children with disabilities.

Marion County officials secretly approved a $2.4 billion data center during a winter storm, with the project listed on the agenda only as 'Project Liberty' under a nondisclosure agreement. Residents were not informed of the project details before the vote. Community backlash erupted after investigative reporting revealed the deal, with residents demanding transparency about environmental and economic impacts.

The Sembrando Esperanza Coalition, an El Paso economic and environmental justice group, called on the city, county, and utilities to terminate their contracts with Meta over a proposed $473 million natural gas power plant (the McCloud facility) to power Meta's 1GW, $1.5 billion data center on 1,000 acres in far Northeast El Paso. Organizer Veronica Carbajal said the community was told Meta would rely on renewable energy but 'when we looked at the contracts ourselves, we realized that was an illusion.' El Paso is already ranked among the worst US cities for ozone/smog and has strained water resources.

TenKey LandCo sued Simpson County over a land use ordinance. Franklin's council and planning commission both voted unanimously to reject the developer's rezoning. 150 community members attended the Feb. 5 meeting.

opposition_groups: Deep South Center for Environmental Justice
summary: City Council voted unanimously (6-0) to enact a one-year moratorium on data centers, crypto mining, and server farms while working on zoning definitions.
Planning & Zoning Commission denied Corvus Investment Group's request to rezone 240+ acres to Business Park (which allows data centers) and recommended Light Industrial zoning instead.

Atlas Development proposed 'Project Bus,' a 12-building data center complex with 2 substations on 350 acres of rural land in northern Carroll County near homes and schools. The Carroll County Anti-Data Center Coalition organized door-to-door petitions and rallies. Atlas withdrew the proposal in late January 2026 after strong community opposition, but may return with revised plans.

TenKey LandCo proposed three 200,000 sq-ft data centers with natural gas production on 200 acres in Franklin (pop. ~11,000). The city council and planning commission both unanimously rejected rezoning. TenKey sued Simpson County to overturn a December ordinance requiring heavy industrial zoning and conditional use permits for data centers. The zoning commission has twice tabled votes.

HHHunt withdrew its application for the 400-acre Hunting Hawk Technology Center, a 900MW data center campus with 10 buildings totaling 3.9 million sq ft on the Hunting Hawk Golf Club property near the Wyndham community. The Planning Commission voted to deny the project on Jan. 15 after hundreds of residents rallied outside the county administrative building. The proposal included electrical substations, transmission lines, and private water/sewer systems. HHHunt formally withdrew on Jan. 28. A second data center proposal on Route 33 near the Hanover-Henrico line was also introduced shortly after.

Brennan Investment Group withdrew its $2 billion data center proposal for land south of Penny Road and west of Pond Gate Drive in Barrington Hills, a suburb northwest of Chicago. The withdrawal came 'based on initial feedback' before a scheduled Feb. 2 plan commission meeting. A Change.org petition opposing the project had garnered 1,303 signatures citing concerns about air pollution, water quality, and tax impacts. The village considers the matter closed and canceled the Feb. 2 meeting.
Zoning committee voted 5-3 against a proposed six-month moratorium, then the ZBA recommended CyrusOne receive approval for a $500M data center on 280 acres of farmland. Final decision pending.
SB 1488 would establish a moratorium on new data centers until November 2029 while directing study of impacts on water, utility rates, property values, and siting. Bipartisan support.
SB 232 proposed one-year moratorium on hyperscale data centers (50+ MW). Senate committee voted 5-3 to table it. Sen. Howard said committee sided with industry lobbyists.

project_name: SB 978 - Data Center Community Accountability Act
summary: Senator Sasha Renee Perez introduced SB 978 to ban diesel backup generators at data centers, create a separate electricity rate class for facilities over 75MW, require prevailing wage construction, and mandate upfront payment for transmission upgrades.

UK-based Eneus Energy plans a 2GW, $14B data center campus on 1,785 acres near Harlingen. Residents addressed the City Commission opposing the development, citing water and electricity concerns in the Rio Grande Valley. Harlingen's mayor pushed back, but the city says the site is outside its jurisdiction. The city-owned utility entered an agreement for 4.6M gallons/day of reclaimed wastewater for the project.
Carroll County is weighing a moratorium similar to those adopted by Frederick, Prince George's, Baltimore, and Montgomery counties, as data center opposition spreads across Maryland.

A 1,300 MW data center was proposed for Adams County, which would consume 31 times the county's total current electricity consumption. Residents are raising concerns about water, light pollution, noise, and lack of transparency. Economic Development Director signed NDAs related to the project.

Business owners and citizens of Urbana and Champaign County oppose a proposed data center near a new school, local nursing home, and condominium residences. Cedar Bog Nature Preserve has publicly supported the opposition effort. The petition highlights quality of life impacts including noise, air quality, and traffic concerns.

A proposed 26MW Solstice data center campus in Allen Park, Michigan has been postponed after calls for greater scrutiny. A petition gathered 1,535+ signatures, protesters rallied outside city hall, and a second petition targets non-residents in surrounding Wayne County cities. Despite a Q&A session, opposition remains fierce.

Rep. Ruwa Romman introduced HB 1012, a statewide moratorium on new data center construction until March 1, 2027. The bill is part of a wave of bipartisan Georgia legislation targeting data centers. A separate bill stalled after a last-minute amendment opposed by industry gained committee support. At least 60+ local moratorium actions have been introduced across Georgia towns and counties.

Montgomery County Council voted unanimously to pause data center permitting through end of 2026, requiring a study on how to regulate placement and construction. A proposed data center complex in Dickerson at the former coal plant site near the Potomac River drew fierce opposition at a packed town hall.

Baltimore County Council unanimously passed a bill to temporarily freeze data center development, blocking permits until the Planning Board studies potential environmental and economic impacts. The moratorium may last through end of 2026.

Maumee City Council approved a 12-month moratorium on data center construction, joining a wave of northwest Ohio communities pausing data center development while studying environmental and economic impacts.

The Ashville Village Council voted 4-2 to deny EdgeConneX's annexation request for a 490-acre parcel needed for a 680-acre, 1.8GW data center campus about 17 miles south of Columbus. EdgeConneX already owns the adjacent 200-acre parcel and planned 18 major structures including a 1.8GW substation. The village had previously passed a 180-day emergency moratorium on data centers on Dec. 20, 2025. Opposition was fierce since residents became aware of the project in November 2025. The company may still build on the smaller 200-acre site.
After council adopted data center zoning 5-2, Mason Data Center Facts launched a referendum petition targeting 1,200+ signatures to put it to voters.
Village board voted unanimously against annexation for QTS's proposed $12B, 650-acre data center. 'No Data Center in DeForest' gained 4,000+ followers in a village of 10,000.
Farmers filed judicial review against City of Kuna over 3-2 approval (Mayor breaking tie) of a 620-acre data center, citing traffic, farmland loss, and precedent concerns.

The Sterling Heights City Council unanimously voted to impose a one-year moratorium on any new data centers proposed in the city. Officials noted that the city's zoning restrictions, written in 1978, do not reflect modern computing infrastructure. The pause allows the planning and legal departments to study and develop new zoning regulations. Sterling Heights is part of a broader wave across southeast Michigan, where at least 27 communities now have active data center moratoria.
Delaware's DNREC blocked a 6M sq ft, 1.2 GW data center under the Coastal Zone Act due to diesel generators. Would consume power of 875,000-1,000,000 homes. Starwood has appealed.

Delaware's DNREC denied Starwood Digital Ventures' application for a 6-million-sq-ft data center near Delaware City under the Coastal Zone Act, citing 516 backup diesel generators storing 2.5 million gallons of diesel as heavy industry. The 1.2 GW facility could consume as much power as 1 million homes. Starwood appealed on Feb 25, 2026.

Three data center proposals in Columbia County drew 50+ public comments during a 4.5-hour planning meeting, with residents opposing all three. Commissioners approved two (White Oak and Pumpkin Center) but denied one. A citizen filed lawsuits alleging the rezonings were approved without verified water/sewer capacity analysis or completed regional impact review.

opposition_groups: Mt. Orab Residents for Responsible Development
summary: Village Councilman Eric Lang introduced two ordinances for a 180-day data center moratorium amid community outcry over a mysterious 1,000-acre 'mega site' project requesting up to 2,000 MW. A Facebook group grew to 1,700+ members and nearly 1,000 residents signed a petition. Council members had signed NDAs about the project.
State legislators introduced S.9144, a 3+ year statewide moratorium on permits for data centers using 20 MW or more while DEC completes a comprehensive environmental impact statement.

State Sen. Liz Krueger and Assemblymember Anna Kelles introduced S.9144, a three-year moratorium on new data center permitting while DEC completes an environmental impact statement. Over 100 New York organizations endorsed the bill, called the 'strongest data center moratorium in the country.'

Multiple bipartisan Idaho bills target data centers in 2026: HB 656 would sunset data center sales tax exemptions; Rep. Mickelsen's bill would block utilities from connecting 20MW+ users without proving no rate increases for existing customers.

Ohio Democrats introduced a slate of bills to regulate data centers including transparency requirements, environmental standards, and fair taxation. Around 18 municipalities have enacted or are considering moratoriums. A bipartisan Data Center Study Commission has been proposed.

opposition_groups: No Data Centers in the Driftless
summary: An unnamed company is seeking 400-500 acres in Cassville for a $1B data center. Community members launched a 'No Data Centers in the Driftless' campaign to fight the project. The Grant County Economic Development Corp. expects to learn by spring 2026 whether the proposal advances.
Commissioners unanimously denied Talen Energy's request to rezone 800+ acres from agricultural to industrial. Would have allowed Amazon data centers co-located with Talen's gas plant.

Hood County, 40 miles SW of Fort Worth, has at least four proposed data centers with two more incoming. Commissioners voted down a proposed moratorium 3-2, twice, despite 8 hours of public testimony. The Comanche Circle project sits in a recharge zone for the Upper Trinity Aquifer and could use 1M gallons/day. A state senator warned the AG that counties lack authority to impose moratoriums, chilling local opposition.

Dutch tech company Nebius plans a hyperscale data center scaling to 1.2 GW -- 18 times the electricity of all 55,000 Independence households combined. Over 200 residents packed a February meeting to organize opposition. The city council was expected to vote March 2 on a 90% tax abatement worth up to $150.6 billion in industrial revenue bonds.
Board of Aldermen unanimously passed a 12-month moratorium after ~50 residents spoke out. Prompted by inquiries about using the decommissioned paper mill site for a data center.
Lawmakers advanced legislation requiring renewable energy sourcing. Microsoft privately lobbying against provisions. Columbia Riverkeeper cites threats to salmon habitat.
HB 1265 would prohibit new data center construction for one year and establish a study committee on environmental impact.

A proposal to convert a warehouse in Provo's East Bay into a 30MW data center faced opposition from Mayor Judkins, who stated the project was not in residents' best interest. Despite the mayor's objections, the planning commission recommended rezoning approval.

QTS is developing a $2 billion data center at 2883 Gordon Highway between two residential subdivisions in west Augusta. Residents were blindsided by the project and demanded answers about size, traffic, noise, wetlands, and strain on the power grid at a town hall on Feb 11, 2026. Many complained they did not learn about the project until construction had already begun.

Over 200 people testified at a Wisconsin PSC hearing opposing a proposed utility rate structure for data centers, with the vast majority opposing the current proposal. Democratic lawmakers proposed a statewide data center moratorium. A Marquette poll found 70% of WI voters say data center costs outweigh benefits, up from 55% in October.
Rep. Melanie Sachs proposed a moratorium amendment to LD 307 pausing permits for data centers above 20 MW until July 2028.

Colorado's 2026 legislature is debating two competing data center bills: SB 26-102 (backed by 54 environmental groups) requiring 100% renewable energy by 2031, and HB 26-1030 (industry-backed) offering 20-year tax exemptions with weaker guardrails.

Blanchester Village Council unanimously approved a moratorium on data center development via Ordinance 2026.007, citing concerns about being 'circled' by nearby data center proposals in Wilmington and Mt. Orab.

Metrobloks proposed an urban data center on 14 acres at the former Sherman Drive-In site. Residents organized protests citing environmental justice concerns, as research shows Black communities near data centers report worsened air quality. Hearing examiner approved despite opposition; the neighborhood plans to appeal.

Wisconsin Democratic lawmakers introduced statewide data center moratorium legislation on a statewide day of action, proposing to prohibit data center operations until the legislature enacts protections including a statewide planning authority and explicit bans on shifting data center energy and water costs onto residential customers. A separate bill would ban nondisclosure agreements for new data center projects.

The Port of Walla Walla Commissioners unanimously approved selling 553 acres to Amazon Web Services for a $4.8 billion data center campus with 16 warehouses spanning 3.4 million sq ft at the Wallula Gap Business Park. Local opponents, including the College Place Mayor, argued the project threatens the valley's agricultural roots, limited water supplies, and natural beauty. Critics cited water consumption as 'ecologically devastating' in dry Eastern Washington and noted elevated nitrate levels in well water near an existing data center in Boardman. Washington HB 2515 is advancing through the Senate to regulate data center water and energy impacts.
Commissioners unanimously approved a 12-month moratorium on data centers, AI computing, and cryptocurrency mining in unincorporated areas through February 2027.

Preserving Montgomery County filed a lawsuit alleging Sunshine Law violations by county officials in negotiating a massive AWS data center deal. The suit claims the county concealed negotiations, improperly approved up to $35B in industrial revenue bonds, and gave inadequate public notice. Scores of residents packed meetings to oppose the project.
First Minnesota city to enact a data center moratorium. One-year pause on facilities using more than 20 MW or within 500 feet of residential land.
City Council voted 5-2 to deny a $1.5B, 380MW data center after 8+ hours of testimony with 100+ residents speaking. Came amid historic aquifer drops and Stage 4 drought restrictions.

A 720-acre parcel on Coleman Road near Valdosta was rezoned for a potential data center in 2025 without explicit disclosure. The Sierra Club Georgia Chapter called for a moratorium. A town hall organized by Lowndes Citizens Against Data Centers drew strong opposition over water usage, power demand, and impacts on the Withlacoochee River watershed.
Richfield Township Board of Trustees passed a data center moratorium on Feb. 17, joining other northwest Ohio communities in pausing development while studying impacts.

Lackawanna County Commissioner Bill Gaughan called for a three-year statewide moratorium on large-scale data centers. State Sen. Rosemary Brown and State Reps. Kyle Mullins and Kyle Donahue co-sponsored moratorium legislation. HB 2150 requiring yearly energy/water consumption reports advanced through committee. A Quinnipiac poll showed 68% of Pennsylvanians oppose data centers in their community.

Hays County water advocates identified five potential data center developments and organized protests. A proposed 30-day moratorium on water-intensive building permits was tabled after county attorneys warned it would trigger lawsuits. San Marcos City Council voted 5-2 to deny a $1.5B data center after 8+ hours of public testimony with 100+ residents opposing. Aquifers in the region are at historic lows.

xAI purchased a power plant in Southaven and plans to invest $20B+ in data centers in DeSoto County. 27 temporary gas turbines roar 'like jet engines' day and night, causing respiratory problems and insomnia for residents. The Safe and Sound Coalition gathered 900+ signatures demanding shutdown. At a Feb 2026 public hearing, not a single person spoke in favor of xAI's proposed permanent air permit. NAACP sent intent-to-sue notice for Clean Air Act violations.
Monclova Township trustees approved a 12-month moratorium on data centers on Feb. 18, joining Maumee, Waterville, and Richfield Township in northwest Ohio's wave of data center pauses.
City Council voted unanimously to block a data center and remove data centers as a permitted use. Hundreds protested near an elementary school. Council restored plans for a public park.
Iowa bill introduced requiring data centers to report water and energy use, pushing for transparency as the state hosts massive campuses from Microsoft, Meta, Apple, and Google.

Over 1,700 residents signed a petition opposing a potential data center on 500+ acres of prime farmland near Shaker Village at Pleasant Hill. Hundreds packed the Mercer County Fairgrounds to voice unified opposition. The land is close to KU's E.W. Brown power plant and the Kentucky River. No formal application has been submitted but the county hired a consultant to evaluate data center regulations.

South Dakota SB 232 proposed a one-year moratorium on hyperscale data center construction. The committee killed the bill on a 10-2 vote. A separate bill (HB 1301) would have imposed a moratorium while addressing electricity cost allocation. Both were defeated.

The City of Aurora is developing new data center regulations as its existing moratorium nears expiration. The proposed rules include maximum noise levels of 59 dB daytime and 49 dB nighttime, a ban on roof-mounted chiller units within 1,500 feet of residential/educational/hospital uses, and ground-mounted chillers and generators at least 1,000 feet from such uses. The regulations stem from extensive noise complaints by residents near the existing CyrusOne data center. Over 200 residents voiced concerns at an open house on Feb. 12 and at a Planning and Zoning Commission meeting. CyrusOne's permanent sound solution is due by Sept. 30, 2026.
Governor Pritzker proposed two-year pause on data center tax incentives. SB4016 'POWER Act' introduced to limit cost-shifting to residential ratepayers and require clean energy procurement.

Environmental advocates, union reps, engineers, and lawmakers gathered at the State Capitol calling for a two-year moratorium on hyperscale data centers. Sen. Jen McEwen (DFL-Duluth) plans legislation to temporarily halt new permits while the state studies long-term effects. Demands include banning NDAs and requiring environmental impact statements.

Dryden Town Board unanimously banned data centers and cryptocurrency mines, becoming the first town in New York State to outlaw data centers. The ban followed an 18-month land-use moratorium and was driven by concerns over energy draw, water use, and noise.

Denver Mayor Mike Johnston and city council announced plans for a one-year citywide moratorium on new data center construction, likely starting spring 2026. This follows CoreSite skipping a packed Globeville community town hall where hundreds showed up. The moratorium will temporarily prevent CoreSite from building two additional planned data center buildings but does not affect the first phase already under construction. Senator Bernie Sanders praised Denver's move and renewed his call for a national moratorium.

Mayor Mike Johnston announced a one-year moratorium on new data center construction. The city will review zoning, water, energy use, and affordability impacts amid opposition in Globeville-Elyria-Swansea.

Lincoln County Commissioner proposed a 1-year moratorium on hyperscale data centers requiring 100+ MW, but no commissioner seconded the motion, killing the measure. The commission voted 3-2 to refer regulations to Planning and Zoning.

Sunbury residents packed overflow rooms to oppose Amazon's proposed $2 billion data center that would rezone 330+ acres of farmland for industrial use. Public hearing postponed to March 23 after city discovered parcel number errors. Residents cited water quality, environmental, and infrastructure concerns.

Cobb County Board of Commissioners voted unanimously to implement a 180-day moratorium on new data center permits and rezoning applications in unincorporated areas. Resolution cited noise pollution, air quality impacts from cooling systems and backup generators, aesthetics concerns, and significant effects on power and water utilities.

Franklin County Commissioners voted unanimously to maintain a year-long data center moratorium preventing applications until February 24, 2027. The moratorium gives the county time to gather information and develop a new zoning code for data center infrastructure.

South Dakota Senate defeated both pro- and anti-data center bills in a chaotic session, with a missing senator contributing to the outcome. Defeated bills included tax rebate incentives, backup generator permitting, a one-year moratorium, 45-decibel noise limits, and one-mile residential setback requirements. Neither supporters nor skeptics got what they wanted.

More than 200 people from 40 Indiana counties gathered at the Indiana State Library on February 24 to discuss data center impacts. Citizens Action Coalition is calling for a full statewide moratorium on data center developments until regulations are in place. State lawmakers took little legislative action despite growing community concern across the state.
Portage enacted a temporary moratorium on data centers and battery storage facilities through end of year.
Beltline Energy withdrew its $16B data center rezoning request minutes into a packed meeting. Missouri Dept. of Conservation flagged the site for endangered species habitat.

The GES Coalition delivered a 'Good Neighbor Proposal' to CoreSite opposing their DE3 expansion in Elyria-Swansea, one of Denver's most environmentally burdened neighborhoods. Over 300 individuals and 11 organizations signed demanding transparency on energy/water use.

Phillipsburg Town Council voted unanimously to approve first reading of an ordinance banning data centers in all zones, citing water and energy demands. Final vote and public hearing scheduled for March 10, 2026. Officials referenced the town's Master Plan prioritizing rural character and agricultural heritage.

Dearborn County Board of Commissioners approved a one-year moratorium on rezoning applications for data centers, commercial solar, and battery storage facilities. Over 1,700 people signed an online petition calling for the moratorium. Planning commission will begin considering rule changes in April.

project_name: White Oak Business Park / Pumpkin Center
summary: Citizen filed two judicial review petitions on Feb. 25 challenging Columbia County Board of Commissioners' approval of data center rezonings for White Oak Business Park and Pumpkin Center projects. Petitions allege procedural defects, incomplete Development of Regional Impact review, failure to notify school district, and lack of verified water/sewer capacity analysis.

Residents protested at the Shreveport Municipal Auditorium after Amazon announced a $12 billion data center network spanning Caddo and Bossier parishes, the company's first in Louisiana. A Caddo Parish commissioner called for a pause on a water service agreement that could draw up to 5 million gallons daily from Caddo Lake. The Caddo Parish Commission considered but declined to vote on a one-year moratorium. Amazon pledged $400 million in water infrastructure improvements and claims their design is 80% more water-efficient than industry average. The project would support 540 full-time and 1,700 indirect jobs.
City Council approved a six-month moratorium 5-2 to study impacts on water, energy, design standards, setbacks, and noise.
Bipartisan legislators introduced a three-bill package to halt all state and local data center permits until April 2027. At least 27 Michigan communities already have local moratoria.

Florida Senate unanimously passed restrictions requiring companies to pay for all utilities, a 5-mile buffer from schools/residential zones, banning NDAs between companies and governments, and requiring public disclosure.

Sen. Blumenthal introduced bipartisan GRID Act — first federal bill on data centers. Requires off-grid power, $1M/day fines for grid violations, project labor agreements, 10-year transition for existing facilities.

Penzance Management announced a $4B data center in Berkeley County, the first certified 'high-impact' project under WV's HB 2014. The project is exempt from local zoning, noise, lighting, and land use ordinances. Residents and advocacy groups raised concerns that long-term environmental costs outweigh short-term revenue, and that the law denies communities any voice in siting decisions. Lawmakers blocked multiple amendments to restore local control or require water usage reporting.

Missouri Reps. Colin Wellenkamp and Mike Costlow filed the AI Infrastructure, Grid Integrity and Water Resource Protection Act requiring large-load consumers like data centers to pay for electricity and grid infrastructure costs rather than passing them to ratepayers.

Spring Hill approved rezoning of 316 acres from Rural Residential to General Industrial, potentially for a data center. About 300 homes would be impacted. Residents plan to fight the rezoning at a public hearing rescheduled to March 25, 2026.

A bipartisan group of Michigan House members introduced the three-bill Data Center Regulation Act to halt new data center approvals until April 2027. At least 27 Michigan communities have active moratoria. Gov. Whitmer opposes the bills, saying legislation preventing economic growth would be vetoed.

Bipartisan group of Michigan House lawmakers introduced a three-bill package to temporarily halt all new data center development statewide until April 1, 2027. At least 27 Michigan communities now have active local moratoria in place while drafting zoning rules. Bills would prevent state EGLE and local governments from issuing data center permits.

Missouri lawmakers filed the AI Infrastructure, Grid Integrity and Water Resource Protection Act, requiring large-load consumers like data centers to pay for grid infrastructure upgrades (not passed to consumers) and obtain DNR permits for using more than 2 million gallons of fresh water per day. Senator Josh Hawley also called for tech companies to pay for their energy and water usage.
TigerDC withdrew its $3B Project Spero after the County Council voted to deny tax incentives. Hundreds packed council chambers. 1,200+ petition signatures against.

The Henrico Board of Zoning Appeals unanimously denied Centra Logistics' vested-rights appeal to build a 1 million sq ft data center complex on Darbytown Road in Varina. The BZA upheld the December 2025 decision by the county Planning Director, finding Centra had not received a 'significant affirmative governmental act,' was not the landowner when it filed its claim, and had not obtained the provisional-use permit now required under Henrico's June 2025 ordinance. Residents of the nearby New Market Village subdivision strongly opposed the project. Centra may appeal to Henrico Circuit Court.

Scranton Materials LLC filed an appeal after the Ransom Township Board of Supervisors rejected its zoning amendment application that would have permitted data centers at 819 Newton Road. The company argues the township's zoning ordinance unlawfully excludes data centers, claiming Pennsylvania municipalities must allow every type of lawful land use somewhere within their borders. The proposal drew dozens of residents and elected officials who opposed the plans at multiple public hearings. The township's existing noise and building height requirements were cited as too restrictive for data centers.

Marana residents filed a lawsuit against the Town of Marana after the Town Clerk rejected referendum petitions with over 2,800 signatures that sought to force a public vote on the town council's Jan. 6 unanimous rezoning approval for Amazon's Project Blue data center on two 300-acre parcels. The petitions were invalidated on a technicality -- they omitted legal property descriptions required for zoning ordinances. A Pima County judge heard arguments on March 4. Three residents also launched town council campaigns in response. Separately, the No Desert Data Center Coalition filed suit against Pima County for approving a related land sale and rezoning.
Facing its third data center, Linn County rolled out extensive zoning rules with a new exclusive-use district requiring setbacks, noise limits, traffic studies, and emergency planning.

Mason County planning commission approved an ordinance paving the way for a 2,000-acre data center by an undisclosed company, despite significant opposition from residents and the We Are Mason County Ky. grassroots group. The ordinance requires 750-foot setbacks from residences, limits noise to 50 dB, and restricts construction to daytime hours. Opponents cite loss of prime farmland and secretive NDA-protected planning.

Linn County passed a restrictive ordinance requiring data center builders to conduct environmental reviews, limit light pollution and noise, and compensate the county for infrastructure costs. Google announced plans for a third data center in unincorporated Linn County in October 2025, prompting the regulatory response.

SELC and Sierra Club filed lawsuit challenging Georgia EPD's air permit allowing Georgia Power to add four gas- and oil-fired turbines at Plant Bowen to serve data center power demand. Environmental groups argue the new units would release smog threatening air quality for millions of Atlanta-area residents. The case was filed before Georgia Office of State Administrative Hearings.

Rezoning request to allow data centers on 460 acres of rural land in Hollenback Township was deemed denied and is now heading to Luzerne County Court. Residents packed meetings to demand answers about data center impacts. Landowners were reportedly offered $175K per acre, causing a divisive community split.

Over 100 New York groups called for passage of the statewide three-year data center moratorium bill, the 'strongest-in-the-nation' proposal that would prevent construction of new data centers while state agencies study environmental and economic impacts. Food & Water Watch coordinated the coalition effort.

The Charles County Planning Commission voted to recommend denial of Zoning Text Amendment #25-187, which would have established zoning regulations for data centers in the county. Chairman Kevin Wedding cited concerns about higher electric bills for residents. Board member Denard Earl noted that Maryland is conducting a statewide data center impact study expected to conclude in September 2026, suggesting the county wait for those results. Board member Semia Hackett questioned whether approving one facility could open the door to additional projects. The recommendation now moves to the Board of Commissioners for final consideration.

Fulton County commissioners voted 2-1 to approve a one-year moratorium on data center construction, effective immediately. The vote followed a 6-1 recommendation from the Area Plan Commission on Feb. 10 after a standing-room-only public hearing. The moratorium targets a proposed 500MW data center by Decennial Group on 300+ acres near the town of Akron. Community members packed hearings expressing concerns about water and electricity strain. A data center committee will study economic and environmental impacts to guide future county regulations.
